title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: Complete the text with ONE word only.

content.text: Seldom (0) researchers agree so readily on the mechanisms underlying cognitive bias, a phenomenon that continues to puzzle even seasoned psychologists. Although numerous studies attempt to isolate the variables responsible, many fail to capture the subtle interplay between memory, attention and expectation. || Not (9) researchers anticipated, however, was the extent to which individuals rely on mental shortcuts, some of (10) distort incoming information in predictable ways. Albeit (11) these distortions may appear minor, they accumulate over time, shaping decisions in ways that remain largely unnoticed. || Of particular interest is the role of social proof, a principle according to which people adjust their behaviour provided (12) they perceive others doing the same. At a loss (13) to explain why this mechanism exerts such influence, some theorists argue that humans are evolutionarily predisposed to prioritise group consensus. || Each (14) of these explanations highlights a different facet of the issue, yet none fully accounts for the resilience of biased thinking. In (15) many cases, individuals cling to familiar interpretations lest (16).

example.text: Seldom (0) researchers agree so readily on the mechanisms underlying cognitive bias, a phenomenon that continues to puzzle even seasoned psychologists.

questions.explanation: Used in the fixed expression 'Not as researchers anticipated', introducing a comparison between expectation and reality.
Relative pronoun introducing a defining relative clause referring to 'shortcuts'.
Connector meaning 'although', introducing a concessive clause.
Subordinator in the structure 'provided that', expressing a condition.
Preposition completing the fixed phrase 'at a loss to explain'.
Quantifier referring individually to the explanations mentioned.
Adverb functioning as a degree marker in the expression 'in so many cases'.
Pronoun referring back to 'individuals', completing the clause 'lest they...'.
